name: Information Systems (IS)
description: This is your primary disciplinary home. TPC (task–technology fit), ECM, TAM, continuance intention are all core IS theories. Most of your citations will likely come from here.

journals.name: Information & Management
journals.note: Highest volume of TTF, ECM, and continuance studies; most likely home for your eventual publication.

journals.name: Journal of the Association for Information Systems (JAIS)
journals.note: Open-access, with a strong qualitative IS tradition.

journals.name: MIS Quarterly (MISQ)
journals.note: Gold standard; read for theory, less likely publication target at PhD stage.

journals.name: European Journal of Information Systems (EJIS)
journals.note: European tradition, strong on context-sensitive IS research.

journals.name: Information Systems Research (ISR)
journals.note: Flagship venue; read seminal pieces, less realistic as a submission target.

journals.name: Journal of Management Information Systems (JMIS)
journals.note: Flagship venue; read seminal pieces, less realistic as a submission target.

name: ICT4D
description:
As discussed — your broader contextual umbrella. Positions your work in the developing country / Global South discourse.

ICT4D isn't just a "contextual umbrella" — for your novelty claim (professionals in SSA, not smallholder farmers), it's actually where your gap lives. The ICT4D literature has overwhelmingly focused on farmers and rural beneficiaries; your paper is partly a critique of that bias. That makes ICT4D not just context, but a conversation partner.

journals.name: Electronic Journal of Information Systems in Developing Countries (EJISDC)
journals.note: Open-access, African context well-represented; your most likely publication target.

journals.name: Information Technologies & International Development (ITID)
journals.note: MIT Press; strong on development + digital.

journals.name: African Journal of Information Systems (AJIS)
journals.note: Underused but genuinely relevant; SSA-specific IS research.

name: Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Science
description: Agritech is inherently about agricultural productivity and decision-making. Journals in this space increasingly publish on digital tool adoption.

journals.name: Computers and Electronics in Agriculture
journals.note: Largest volume of digital tool adoption studies in ag; directly relevant.

journals.name: Agricultural Systems
journals.note: Broader systems thinking; useful for framing platform use in research workflows.

journals.name: Food Policy
journals.note: If your work touches on policy dimensions of ag data infrastructure.

journals.name: World Development (also crosses into development studies)
NJAS — Wageningen Journal of Life Sciences

name: Development Studies / Development Economics
description: Concerned with poverty, smallholder farming, rural livelihoods. Your SSA context plugs directly into this literature.

name: Innovation & Entrepreneurship
description: Agritech is a growing startup ecosystem in SSA. Product discovery connects to innovation diffusion (Rogers' Diffusion of Innovations theory is relevant here).

name: Marketing & Consumer Behavior
description: Continuance intention has strong parallels with customer retention, loyalty, and repeat purchase behavior. Product discovery overlaps with consumer search behavior and decision journeys.
journals: Journal of the Academy of Marketing Science
Journal of Interactive Marketing (relevant for digital products)

name: Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)
description: Concerned with usability, user experience, and interface design. Closely related to why tools fit or don't fit user tasks.
journals: ACM CHI Conference Proceedings — the premier HCI venue (conference, not journal)

name: Sociology / Rural Sociology
description: Social dynamics, community influence, gender dimensions of technology adoption in SSA.
journals: African Sociological Review (relevant for SSA context)

name: Behavioral Science / Psychology
description: Underpins most IS models — TAM, ECM draw heavily on psychology of behavior and motivation.
journals: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes (very relevant — bridges psychology and management)
Computers in Human Behavior (highly relevant — sits between IS and psychology)

name: Public Policy / e-Government
description: If your agritech tools involve government or NGO-driven initiatives, this field becomes relevant.
journals: Government Information Quarterly (GIQ) — most directly relevant
Journal of Public Administration Research and Theory
Transforming Government: People, Process and Policy

name: Science & Technology Studies (STS) / Research Infrastructure
description: Your users are scientists — breeders, researchers — using platforms for research data collection. A niche literature covers research information systems, data infrastructure in science, and digital tools in agricultural R&D; CGIAR itself has published in this space. Genuinely peripheral, but it opens access to a body of work that almost no TTF paper has touched — a differentiator.
journals: Journal of the Association for Information Science and Technology (JASIST)
CGIAR working papers and institutional publications
